HR596.reason: Full repeal of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) and related provisions in the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010

HR3762.reason: Budget reconciliation bill that repealed key ACA provisions including the individual mandate, Medicaid expansion, and defunded Planned Parenthood - vetoed by Obama

HR1190.reason: Protecting Seniors' Access to Medicare Act - repealed the Independent Payment Advisory Board (IPAB) created by the ACA to control Medicare spending growth

HR986.reason: Protecting Americans with Preexisting Conditions Act - strengthened ACA protections for people with preexisting conditions by blocking Trump administration efforts to weaken these guarantees

HRES826.reason: Resolution expressing disapproval of the Trump administration's harmful actions towards Medicaid - voting Yea supports maintaining Medicaid protections established under the ACA

HR498.reason: Do No Harm in Medicaid Act (119th) - despite its name, cuts Medicaid by reducing the federal matching rate (FMAP) for expansion states from 90% to 80% for states that cover certain immigrants, undermining ACA Medicaid expansion

HR6703.reason: Lower Health Care Premiums for All Americans Act (119th) - Republican bill that restructures ACA marketplace rules, loosening essential health benefit requirements and allowing cheaper plans with fewer protections

HR1.reason: 119th Congress reconciliation bill that reduces the FMAP for Medicaid expansion to 80% in states providing coverage to certain immigrants, cutting ACA Medicaid expansion funding

S3385.reason: Bill to extend the enhancement of the health care premium tax credit that was expanded under the ACA - voting Yea continues making ACA marketplace coverage more affordable

S3386.reason: Republican alternative to ACA premium subsidies using Health Savings Accounts - shifts from ACA model of comprehensive coverage to HSA-based approach that favors healthy and wealthy individuals

SJRES84.reason: CRA disapproval of CMS rule on 'Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act; Market Integrity and Affordability' - the underlying Trump-era rule weakened ACA marketplace protections, so disapproving it supports the ACA

SJRES32.reason: CRA disapproval of CMS COVID-19 healthcare staff vaccination mandate - while not directly about the ACA, opposing vaccine mandates for healthcare facilities undermines public health infrastructure supported by the ACA

HR3.reason: Elijah E. Cummings Lower Drug Costs Now Act - allows Medicare to negotiate drug prices, building on ACA framework to make healthcare more affordable
